install.pipx (all platforms — recommended): python -m pip install --user pipx && pipx install aider-chat
install.WARNING: Aider requires Python 3.10–3.12. If `python` is missing or older, install it first (winget Python.Python.3.12 / brew install python@3.12 / apt install python3-pip). pipx is preferred over plain pip because it isolates aider into its own venv and puts it on PATH automatically.
install.note: After install, verify with `aider --version`. The first run will pick up API credentials from environment vars (OPENAI_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) or from ~/.aider.conf.yml — Echobird's 'Update model config' flow writes the YAML for you.

install_flow.summary: Detect Python 3.10+ → install pipx if missing → pipx install aider-chat → verify
agent_steps: Detect OS and verify Python ≥ 3.10 with `python --version` / `python3 --version`. If missing or older, install Python first via the platform's package manager (winget Python.Python.3.12, brew install python@3.12, apt install python3 python3-pip, etc.).
Prefer pipx — `python -m pip install --user pipx` then `python -m pipx ensurepath`, then `pipx install aider-chat`.
If pipx isn't available and the user is OK with a global pip install, `python -m pip install --user aider-chat` works.
After install, run `aider --version` to confirm. If the command isn't found, instruct the user to restart their shell so PATH updates take effect.
Tell the user that model selection happens via Echobird's App Manager — clicking 'Update model config' writes ~/.aider.conf.yml with the chosen base URL / API key / model.
tell_user: Python prerequisite (and how to install if missing)
That `aider` is now on PATH (may need a shell restart)
That ~/.aider.conf.yml is the config file Echobird writes when applying a model

identity_guard.description: Aider is an open-source CLI coding agent (paul-gauthier / Aider-AI on GitHub) that supports OpenAI, Anthropic, and many OpenAI-compatible endpoints. It is NOT Aider Pro / aider.app / any commercial fork — install ONLY via pip/pipx/brew from the canonical `aider-chat` package.
